data.title: Daiichi Sankyo Advances ADC Pipeline With New Esophageal Cancer Study
data.description:
Daiichi Sankyo Company (DSKYF) announced an update on their ongoing clinical study.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The Phase 2 study “A Phase 2 Open-Label, Umbrella Platform Design Study of Investigational Agent(s) in Participants With 2L/3L Unresectable Locally Advanced or Metastatic Esophageal Cancer: KEYMAKER-U06 Substudy 06F” tests if ifinatamab deruxtecan can help people with advanced esophageal squamous cell cancer. It focuses on tumor response in patients who have already tried other treatments, a high-need group with few options.

The trial evaluates ifinatamab deruxtecan, also called I-DXd, an antibody-drug conjugate given by IV infusion every three weeks. It also allows standard rescue drugs to manage nausea and other side effects so participants can stay on treatment longer and show a clearer benefit-risk profile.

The design is interventional with a single treatment arm and no randomization, so everyone receives I-DXd rather than standard care or placebo. The study is open label with no masking, and the main goal is to see if the drug shrinks or clears tumors in this late-line esophageal cancer setting.

The study was first submitted on 5 February 2026 as the recruiting phase began, marking the start of patient enrollment. The latest update was filed on 15 April 2026, signaling protocol updates and confirming the trial remains active and relevant for tracking pipeline progress.

For investors, this update adds another late-stage asset to Daiichi Sankyo’s growing antibody-drug conjugate franchise, which already drives much of its valuation. Positive data here could support new indications, strengthen bargaining power with partners like Merck, and intensify competition with ADC players in gastrointestinal cancers such as AstraZeneca and Seagen.

In the near term, the stock impact may be modest as the study is still early, but regular updates can support sentiment around pipeline depth and deal potential. Investors should watch for interim response data and safety trends, as strong signals could lift expectations for long-term revenue and strategic partnerships in the ADC space.

This study is currently recruiting and remains active, with full details and ongoing updates available on the ClinicalTrials.gov portal.

To learn more about DSKYF’s potential, visit the Daiichi Sankyo Company drug pipeline page.

data.description:

AVRO India Ltd. ( (IN:AVROIND) ) has issued an update.

Avro India Ltd. informed exchanges that it convened an Extraordinary General Meeting on April 18, 2026, conducted entirely via video conferencing and other audio-visual means in line with Companies Act and SEBI regulations. The company detailed compliance with MCA and SEBI circulars, including electronic dispatch of the EGM notice to eligible shareholders and restricting attendance to direct participants without proxies.

The meeting, which ran from 1:00 p.m. to 1:28 p.m. IST, recorded the presence of 32 members along with the board’s key directors, the chief financial officer, the company secretary, and the secretarial auditor. With quorum confirmed and the chairman presiding, the orderly conduct of the EGM underscores the company’s adherence to mandated governance processes and remote participation norms for listed entities.

The latest announcement is out from Tata Capital Ltd. ( (IN:TATACAP) ).

Tata Capital Limited has scheduled a media conference call on 23 April 2026 at 5:30 p.m. IST to discuss its audited standalone and consolidated financial results for the quarter and year ended 31 March 2026. Lupin Limited has reported that the U.S. Food and Drug Administration has completed an inspection of its manufacturing facility in Somerset, New Jersey, conducted from April 13 to April 17, 2026, and closed the review with a Form 483 containing three observations. Kanoria Chemicals & Industries Ltd ( (IN:KANORICHEM) ) has issued an announcement.

Kanoria Chemicals & Industries Ltd has approved the allotment of 7% non-convertible, non-cumulative, non-participating, redeemable preference shares with a face value of Rs. 1,000 each. The issue, structured as a preferential allotment on a private placement basis, will see 495,000 such shares issued to promoter group entity R. V. The move represents a targeted capital-raising step that strengthens the company’s funding base without diluting equity shareholding. By channeling funds from a promoter group entity via redeemable preference shares, Kanoria Chemicals reinforces promoter support while optimizing its capital structure, a development that may be viewed positively by creditors and other stakeholders.

Kanoria Chemicals & Industries Ltd is an Indian chemical manufacturer based in Kolkata, engaged in producing industrial chemicals and related products for diverse end-use sectors. data.title: Coforge Sees Senior M&A and Investor Relations Leaders Step Down After Major Expansion Drive
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Coforge Limited ( (IN:COFORGE) ) has provided an update.

Coforge Limited has announced the resignation of Anup Kumar, Executive Vice President and Global Head of M&A, and Manish Hemrajani, Head of Investor Relations, both effective at the close of business on April 17, 2026 in the U.S. The management changes come after a period in which Coforge executed five acquisitions, integrated Cigniti, divested its non-core AdvantageGo business, and scaled revenues from $1.1 billion in May 2025 to $2.5 billion post-Encora, underscoring a significant M&A-driven expansion phase whose leadership will now transition.

In his resignation note, Kumar highlighted that his decision stems from a desire to pursue an entrepreneurial path, while expressing confidence in Coforge’s continued growth as the Encora deal nears closing following regulatory approvals. The twin departures mark a shift in senior leadership at a time when the company is digesting major transactions and elevated scale, making succession in M&A and investor relations critical for sustaining its growth trajectory and maintaining market confidence.

More about Coforge Limited

Coforge Limited is an India-headquartered IT services and digital transformation company serving global enterprises across sectors. The firm focuses on technology-led solutions, including application development, integration, and specialized domain platforms, and has expanded its scale through a combination of organic growth and strategic acquisitions in key international markets.

data.title: Polestar Earnings Call: Growth Surges Amid Heavy Losses
data.description:
Polestar Automotive Holding UK PLC Sponsored ADR Class C ((PSNYW)) has held its Q4 earnings call. Read on for the main highlights of the call.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Polestar Automotive’s latest earnings call painted a mixed picture for investors. Management highlighted record volumes, strong revenue growth and notable progress on adjusted margins, but these achievements were overshadowed by heavy impairment charges, persistent GAAP losses and sizable cash burn, leaving the turnaround story promising yet clearly risky.

Record Retail Sales and Volume Growth

Polestar delivered more than 60,100 cars in FY2025, up 34% year on year and squarely within its 30%–35% guidance range. Q4 retail sales climbed 27% to about 15,600 units, while Q1 2026 reached roughly 13,100 cars, marking the company’s strongest first quarter ever.

Strong Revenue Growth

Revenue exceeded USD 3.0 billion in FY2025, a surge of 50% from the prior year driven by higher volumes, a richer model mix and rising carbon credit income. Q4 revenue jumped 54% year on year to USD 887 million, underscoring solid commercial traction despite tough market conditions.

Improvement in Adjusted Profitability Metrics

Adjusted gross margin for 2025 improved sharply to -0.7% from -12.5% in 2024, with Q4 margins turning positive at 2% versus a deeply negative level a year earlier. Adjusted EBITDA loss narrowed by about USD 300 million to -USD 783 million for the year, and Q4 adjusted EBITDA improved to -USD 223 million from -USD 470 million.

Capital Raises and Strong Shareholder Support

The company secured about USD 1.2 billion of new equity between June 2025 and March 2026, including roughly USD 1.0 billion since December. Equity issued at USD 19.34 per share boosted free float above 40%, while plans to convert around USD 640 million of shareholder loans into equity aim to bolster liquidity and reduce leverage.

Network Expansion and Distribution Momentum

Polestar expanded its retail footprint by about 50% in 2025, growing from 140 to 210 sales points and targeting roughly 250 by year end, implying another 20% increase. The Polestar 4 has emerged as the volume engine, accounting for about 65%–66% of recent sales and anchoring the brand in key markets.

Product and Technology Advancements

Management touted its largest product offensive yet, with four new models planned over the next three years, including the Polestar 5, a new Polestar 4 variant, a next‑generation Polestar 2 and the compact Polestar 7. Upgrades to the Polestar 3, such as an 800V architecture enabling up to 350–500 kW charging and a major NVIDIA compute jump, seek to keep the brand technologically competitive.

Cost Discipline and Product Cost Reductions

Headcount fell by about 25% in 2025, contributing to SG&A savings of USD 34 million and total cost reductions of roughly USD 100 million, or 12% year on year. Product costs also moved lower, with the Polestar 4 benefiting from low double‑digit percentage cost cuts via negotiations and selective decontenting while maintaining a premium positioning.

Liquidity and Facility Access

Polestar ended December 2025 with around USD 1.2 billion in cash, offering a liquidity cushion amid ongoing losses. The company also secured roughly USD 1.6 billion of new 12‑month term facilities and renewed about USD 3.0 billion of existing lines, staying in covenant compliance and agreeing amendments for 2026.

Large Impairment Charges and Negative GAAP Margins

Despite operational progress, the P&L was hit by about USD 1.1 billion of impairment charges tied to Polestar 2, Polestar 3 and internal projects including Polestar 5. These write‑downs drove GAAP gross margin to a deeply negative 35% for the year and roughly -38% in Q4, masking underlying margin improvements.

Continued Net Losses and Adjusted EBITDA Deficit

Net losses remained heavy, with Q4 alone posting a USD 799 million deficit, largely due to impairments but still reflecting structural unprofitability. Even on an adjusted basis, EBITDA losses of USD 783 million for 2025 and USD 223 million in Q4 point to continued negative operating cash flow and a long path to break-even.

Pressure on Pricing and Market Dynamics

Management acknowledged that pricing pressure and intense competition weighed on margins, even as product mix improved. Higher duties and tariffs lifted production costs and eroded profitability, underscoring the vulnerability of premium EV makers to policy and trade shifts.

U.S. Market Weakness and Regulatory Headwinds

The U.S. business deteriorated, with its share of retail volumes falling to about 7% in 2025 from 14% in 2024. Higher tariffs, changing policies and the loss or reduction of key incentives and credits cut into sales and hurt the North American revenue mix, complicating Polestar’s global growth story.

Rising Selling Costs and Investment Spend

Sales agent compensation increased by USD 65 million in 2025, broadly in line with rising volumes but still contributing to higher selling costs. R&D spending doubled to USD 78 million as Polestar invested more in new programs and capitalized less, lifting near‑term cash outflows but supporting future products.

High Interest Expense and Legacy CapEx Outflows

The company highlighted a substantial interest burden and residual capital expenditure payments from earlier programs that continued to weigh on cash flow in 2025. While planned debt‑to‑equity conversions should ease financing costs over time, these legacy outflows remain a near‑term drag on free cash generation.

Material Impairment Volatility

Q4 impairment charges dropped to USD 340 million from USD 622 million a year earlier, but their recurring scale signaled ongoing asset valuation risks. Management linked this volatility to fluctuating demand, tariffs and regulatory changes, suggesting earnings will remain sensitive to external shocks.

Cash Burn and Working Capital Pressures

Average cash burn ran at about USD 120 million, underlining the company’s reliance on external funding despite efficiency gains. Inventory reductions of roughly 7,000 vehicles helped working capital, but higher receivables and catch‑up payments on payables offset some of the benefit.

Forward-Looking Guidance and Outlook

For 2026, Polestar is guiding to low double‑digit retail sales growth, supported by an expanded network of about 250 sales points and a continued shift toward the Polestar 4 and new models such as the Polestar 5. Management expects similar three‑digit‑million carbon credit revenue, an improved cash burn profile and a stronger balance sheet following equity raises and loan conversions, though risks from competition and regulation persist.

Polestar’s earnings call showed a company pushing hard on growth, technology and cost control while battling sizable losses and volatile impairments. Investors must weigh improving operational trends and strengthened liquidity against ongoing cash burn, U.S. weakness and policy headwinds as the brand navigates a crowded global EV market.
